items.title: Scotiabank Re-Enters Precious Metals Business It Exited in 2020
items.description: Bank of Nova Scotia has re-entered precious metals trading, six years after exiting the sector, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: Qiagen Draws Early Takeover Interest From EQT, Advent
items.description: Qiagen NV, the European molecular testing company, is drawing early takeover interest from firms including EQT AB and Advent, people familiar with the matter said.

items.title: Europe Faces Fresh Volatility After Iran Flare-Up, Officials Say
items.description: Europe’s economy is looking at a fresh bout of uncertainty following Donald Trump’s declaration that the US ceasefire with Iran is over, officials from the region said.

items.title: Nigeria’s $22 Billion Pension Industry Eyes Infrastructure Fund
items.description: Nigeria’s $22 billion pension industry is studying plans to launch a fund later this year to help Africa’s most populous nation upgrade its frayed infrastructure.

items.title: US Jobless Claims Little Changed as Layoffs Remain Limited

items.title: Los Angeles Grade School for Gifted Kids to Borrow $32 Million
items.description: A Los Angeles grade school that requires applicants to have an IQ of 138 or higher is planning to sell $32 million of municipal bonds on Thursday.

items.title: US Jobless Claims Little Changed Last Week Amid Low Layoffs
items.description: Applications for US unemployment benefits were little changed last week, signaling layoffs remain limited.

items.title: Oil Exporters Build Resilience to Soften Blow of Hormuz Disruption
items.description: A second supply crisis in the Middle East may be less severe than what came before.
